steps.title: Identify the three consecutive integers
steps.content: The first integer is $x$. Since the integers are consecutive and ordered from least to greatest, the second integer is $x+1$ and the third integer is $x+2$.

steps.title: Translate the English statement to an inequality
steps.content:
Break down the given statement into parts:
  • The sum of 4 and the second integer → $4 + (x+1)$
  • the product of 17 and the third integer → $17(x+2)$
  • is less than → $<$
Combining these components, the inequality is:
$$4 + (x+1) < 17(x+2)$$
That matches choice B — B is correct.
